_instructions: Review with Harold Nunes before submitting. Verify all DOIs resolve before including in public posts.
title: RFC-ATF-4: Agent Trust Fabric — Proactive Governance Layer: Anticipatory Veto Protocol, Dynamic Semantic Portability, and Structural Shift Detection

creators.name: Nunes, Harold Alberto
creators._note: Add ORCID if available before submission

description:
RFC-ATF-4 specifies the Proactive Governance Layer (PGL) — Layer 6 of the Agent Trust Fabric (ATF) protocol stack — and is the fourth and capstone RFC in the ATF Open Standard series.

RFC-ATF-4 defines three interoperating mechanisms: (1) the Anticipatory Governance Veto Protocol (AGVP), which emits ML-DSA-65 signed ProactiveVetoReceipts before any governance request arrives — closing the detection latency gap identified in ADR-174; (2) the Dynamic Semantic Portability Protocol (DSPP), which enables ATF receipts to be independently assessed in receiving domains whose regulatory context has evolved since issuance, via a unilateral offline RSA computation over public artifacts (no pairwise negotiation required, O(1) per receipt per domain); and (3) the Structural Shift Detector (SSD), which distinguishes sustained drift excursions from fundamental signal-topology changes via the Component Rank Stability Index (CRSI), making recalibration safety a formally tractable decision.

19 formal properties are proved by the OMNIX Formal Verification Suite (OMNIX-FVS-1.0) using the Z3 SMT solver — all return UNSAT. This includes all new invariants introduced in this RFC plus three foundational invariants from RFC-ATF-1 and RFC-ATF-2, providing end-to-end arithmetic verification of the complete ATF stack. Together with TLA+ model-checked properties, OMNIX-FVS-1.0 constitutes the first dual-methodology formally verified AI governance protocol specification (Z3 SMT for arithmetic invariants + TLA+ for state-machine safety).

Includes:
- 19 Z3 SMT proofs (OMNIX-FVS-1.0) — all UNSAT, machine-reproducible in < 200ms
- 108 deterministic conformance vectors organized by protocol module
- Complete implementation at omnix_core/ (Python 3.11)
- Full test suite: pytest tests/test_formal_verification.py (19 assertions)

Formal verification suite: python -m omnix_core.formal_verification.run_all --json

Builds on: RFC-ATF-1 (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20155016) · RFC-ATF-2 (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20241344) · RFC-ATF-3 (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20247342)
notes:
OMNIX QUANTUM LTD · 71-75 Shelton Street, Covent Garden, London WC2H 9JQ, England.

This is the fourth specification in the ATF Open Standard series. RFC-ATF-1/2/3 are published at Zenodo; RFC-ATF-4 supersedes no prior RFC — it extends the protocol stack with Layer 6 (Proactive Governance Layer).

Priority Records: OMNIX-PAR-2026-AGVP-001 · OMNIX-PAR-2026-SSD-001 · OMNIX-PAR-2026-DSPP-001 · OMNIX-PAR-2026-FVS-001

Dual formal verification: TLA+ (state-machine safety) + Z3 SMT 4.x (arithmetic invariants). Machine-checkable proof runner included.
